article_meta.title: Trump strategy document revives Monroe Doctrine, slams EuropeBy Idrees Ali, Matt Spetalnick and James MackenzieDecember

raw_response: {"detections":[{"biasType":"Spin","biasedText":"slams Europe","explanation":"The headline uses the sensational verb 'slams' rather than a neutral term like 'criticizes' or 'questions.' This is the author's own framing choice in the headline, adding an aggressive tone."},{"biasType":"Spin","biasedText":"The document is the latest - and clearest - expression of Trump's desire to shake up the post-World War Two order","explanation":"The phrase 'shake up' is an interpretive, loaded characterization by the authors. A more neutral framing would describe the strategy as 'redefining' or 'revising' U.S. foreign policy posture."},{"biasType":"Opinion Statements Presented as Fact","biasedText":"It comes as Trump has used increasingly racist rhetoric against immigrants of color in the United States.","explanation":"Characterizing Trump's rhetoric as 'racist' is a subjective judgment presented as fact in the authors' own voice. While many critics have described it this way, it is not attributed to any source and is stated as established truth."},{"biasType":"Spin","biasedText":"the administration took a dour view of its traditional allies in Europe","explanation":"The word 'dour' is an interpretive, subjective characterization by the authors that adds a negative editorial spin to the description of the administration's position."},{"biasType":"Mind reading","biasedText":"Trump's desire to shake up the post-World War Two order led by the United States","explanation":"The authors attribute a specific internal 'desire' to Trump — to 'shake up' the post-WWII order — which is an interpretive inference about his motivations rather than a neutral description of what the document states."},{"biasType":"Slant","biasedText":"Since taking office in January, critics have said Trump's rhetoric evokes modern-day imperialism in the Western Hemisphere. He spoke early on, in vague terms, of retaking the Panama Canal and annexing Greenland and Canada.","explanation":"The article prominently features critics' framing of Trump's Western Hemisphere policy as imperialist, but does not give comparable prominence to supporters or defenders of the policy within the article's own structure. Supportive voices appear only briefly via the strategy document's own language."},{"biasType":"Word Choice","biasedText":"Trump has a history of making positive and admiring comments about Russian President Vladimir Putin that have long prompted criticism that he is 'soft on Russia.'","explanation":"Describing Trump's comments as 'admiring' is an editorializing word choice by the authors. While attributed criticism is noted, 'admiring' is the authors' own characterization and carries a loaded connotation suggesting subservience or naivety."}]}

detections.explanation: The headline uses the sensational verb 'slams' rather than a neutral term like 'criticizes' or 'questions.' This is the author's own framing choice in the headline, adding an aggressive tone.

detections.biasedText: The document is the latest - and clearest - expression of Trump's desire to shake up the post-World War Two order
detections.explanation: The phrase 'shake up' is an interpretive, loaded characterization by the authors. A more neutral framing would describe the strategy as 'redefining' or 'revising' U.S. foreign policy posture.

detections.biasedText: It comes as Trump has used increasingly racist rhetoric against immigrants of color in the United States.
detections.explanation: Characterizing Trump's rhetoric as 'racist' is a subjective judgment presented as fact in the authors' own voice. While many critics have described it this way, it is not attributed to any source and is stated as established truth.

detections.biasedText: the administration took a dour view of its traditional allies in Europe
detections.explanation: The word 'dour' is an interpretive, subjective characterization by the authors that adds a negative editorial spin to the description of the administration's position.

detections.biasedText: Trump's desire to shake up the post-World War Two order led by the United States
detections.explanation: The authors attribute a specific internal 'desire' to Trump — to 'shake up' the post-WWII order — which is an interpretive inference about his motivations rather than a neutral description of what the document states.

detections.biasedText: Since taking office in January, critics have said Trump's rhetoric evokes modern-day imperialism in the Western Hemisphere. He spoke early on, in vague terms, of retaking the Panama Canal and annexing Greenland and Canada.
detections.explanation: The article prominently features critics' framing of Trump's Western Hemisphere policy as imperialist, but does not give comparable prominence to supporters or defenders of the policy within the article's own structure. Supportive voices appear only briefly via the strategy document's own language.

detections.biasedText: Trump has a history of making positive and admiring comments about Russian President Vladimir Putin that have long prompted criticism that he is 'soft on Russia.'
detections.explanation: Describing Trump's comments as 'admiring' is an editorializing word choice by the authors. While attributed criticism is noted, 'admiring' is the authors' own characterization and carries a loaded connotation suggesting subservience or naivety.
